mnemonics: Swing Sits Atop AWT: Imagine a SWING set sitting securely on top of an AWT (a wooden platform), showing the foundation relationship.
keyPoints: Swing components are lightweight and painted directly onto canvases using Java code, unlike heavyweight AWT components that rely on the native OS.
Swing provides a richer set of components (like JTable, JTree) and more customization options (pluggable look-and-feel) than AWT.
Despite using some AWT infrastructure (like events and layout managers), Swing is a separate, more advanced GUI toolkit.
commonMistakes: Mixing AWT and Swing components arbitrarily, which can lead to z-order and repainting issues because heavyweight (AWT) and lightweight (Swing) components don't mix well in the same container.
Assuming AWT is obsolete and completely replaced by Swing, when in reality, Swing still relies on core AWT classes like Graphics, Color, and Layout Managers.
